element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Open track with amusement park scenery; designed as entry point
Amusement park landmarks serve as visual navigation aids
Bay Bridge structure introduces technical cornering demands
Hairpin turns require mastered vehicle control
Player can optimize handling for each circuit difficulty
V.R. View System offers 4 camera angles; player chooses preferred viewpoint

typical_relief_triggers: Open road sections in Big Forest
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty curve: beginner relaxation → intermediate technical skill → expert precision demands. Racing format creates natural tension through competitive pacing rather than scripted events.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Big Forest circuit: gentle, open layout with amusement park scenery; teaches basic racing mechanics in safe environment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Bay Bridge circuit: intermediate difficulty featuring the bay bridge structure itself as defining environmental feature; introduces moderate technical cornering
difficulty_curve.late_game: Acropolis circuit: expert level with tight hairpin turns demanding precision vehicle control and mastered handling
notable_spikes: Transition from Big Forest to Bay Bridge: shift to structured environmental obstacles
Transition to Acropolis: hairpin turn intensity requires significant skill jump

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of three circuits defines its difficulty tier through a signature environmental feature. Beginner has amusement park scenery (visual variety, safe layout); Intermediate features Bay Bridge (structural challenge); Expert centers on hairpin turns at Acropolis. This makes difficulty progression immediately readable through environment.

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear Progression with Three Distinct Challenges
notable_level_patterns.description: Racing game uses three-track structure aligned to arcade difficulty conventions. Each track introduces one primary mechanic layer: basic control, moderate obstacle navigation, expert precision. No backtracking or optional paths—pure forward progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All three circuits follow skill-gated progression model

notable_level_patterns.description: V.R. View System offers 4 camera angle choices at start, allowing players to self-select difficulty through perspective. Different views provide different spatial information and handling feel, enabling skill-matching without separate difficulty modes.
